generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors internal temperature of the styrofoam box near the heatbed with ±0.5°C accuracy using 1-Wire bus protocol (DS18B20 or equivalent). Part of the low-latency sensing infrastructure for real-time control and model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors internal temperature of the styrofoam box at a secondary location (opposite side of heatbed) with ±0.5°C accuracy using 1-Wire bus protocol (DS18B20 or equivalent). Supports uniform thermal distribution validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures ambient external temperature outside the styrofoam box to account for environmental influences on thermal behavior. Uses 1-Wire bus protocol with ±0.5°C accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from InternalTemperatureSensor1 transmitted via RabbitMQ message broker. Periodically polled by low-level driver with sub-second frequency for real-time control."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from InternalTemperatureSensor2 transmitted via RabbitMQ message broker. Periodically polled by low-level driver with sub-second frequency for thermal distribution analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw ambient temperature readings from ExternalTemperatureSensor transmitted via RabbitMQ message broker. Used for environmental compensation in thermal models."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral thermal simulation model with 2 free parameters designed to predict internal temperature dynamics. Less computationally intensive but less accurate for thermal inertia representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral thermal simulation model with 4 free parameters empirically determined to better predict thermal inertia of the heatbed. More accurate but computationally intensive."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data from all sensors (internal/external) preprocessed by Raspberry Pi controller. Used as input for model calibration and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, AnomalyDetectionEnabler
]

instance CalibratedModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized parameter sets for both 2-parameter and 4-parameter thermal models generated through automated calibration algorithms. Ensures alignment between DT predictions and PT behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler, SupervisoryControlEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded controller executing real-time data acquisition, control logic (bang-bang controller), and communication with sensors/actuators via GPIO interfaces. Implements safe protocol to prevent unsafe conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, SupervisoryControlService, DataPreprocessingService
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker facilitating inter-component communication between Raspberry Pi controller, sensors, and DT components. Ensures synchronized data exchange via message-based protocol."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIngestionService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom calibration algorithms adjusting model parameters (2-parameter and 4-parameter) using real-time sensor data. Ensures continuous alignment between DT predictions and PT behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService, PredictiveThermalSimulationService
]

instance PredictiveThermalSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing calibrated thermal models (2-parameter and 4-parameter) to predict internal temperature dynamics under varying conditions. Supports what-if analysis and control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveThermalSimulationService, WhatIfAnalysisService
]

instance AnomalyDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Fault detection algorithms comparing DT predictions against real-time sensor data to identify deviations (e.g., sensor faults, thermal drift). Triggers recalibration or controller adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance SupervisoryControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control logic adjusting heatbed/fan operations dynamically based on calibrated model predictions. Implements bang-bang control strategy for energy-efficient temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:enables SupervisoryControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous ingestion of sensor data (internal/external temperatures) from the Physical Twin for state estimation and system monitoring. Enables real-time feedback for control and calibration."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Identifies deviations between DT predictions and PT behavior using calibrated models. Detects sensor faults, thermal drift, and other anomalies requiring corrective actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight, TriggerRecalibrationAction
]

instance PredictiveThermalSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes calibrated thermal models (2-parameter and 4-parameter) to predict internal temperature dynamics under varying conditions (e.g., environmental changes, content load)."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight, ThermalInertiaInsight
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Evaluates impact of changes (e.g., environmental temperature, content load) on system performance using predictive thermal models. Supports optimization of control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioAnalysisInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automatically refines model parameters using runtime data to improve accuracy for control and predictive tasks. Ensures continuous alignment between DT and PT."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance SupervisoryControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adjusts heatbed/fan operations dynamically based on calibrated model predictions to maintain optimal temperature regulation and energy efficiency. Implements bang-bang control strategy."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationInsight, EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance DataPreprocessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates, formats, and aggregates raw sensor data before transmission to RabbitMQ. Ensures data integrity and system safety through embedded safe protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessedSensorDataInsight
]

instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ingests raw sensor data from RabbitMQ message broker for processing and analysis by the Digital Twin components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessedSensorDataInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current and historical temperature readings (internal/external) for monitoring system state and detecting deviations from expected behavior."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies (e.g., sensor faults, thermal drift) with severity levels and potential root causes based on model-PT comparison."
]

instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted internal temperature trajectories under current and future conditions using calibrated 2-parameter and 4-parameter thermal models."
]

instance ThermalInertiaInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of heatbed thermal inertia effects on system response time and stability, derived from 4-parameter model predictions."
]

instance ScenarioAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Impact analysis of environmental changes (e.g., external temperature fluctuations) or content load variations on system performance using predictive models."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics comparing DT predictions against PT behavior, including parameter confidence intervals and calibration error bounds."
]

instance ControlOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommended adjustments to heatbed/fan operation schedules for optimal temperature regulation and energy efficiency based on model predictions."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Energy consumption estimates and optimization recommendations derived from supervisory control actions and thermal model predictions."
]

instance ProcessedSensorDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validated and formatted sensor data ready for model input, including quality flags and metadata for traceability."
]

instance TriggerRecalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Initiates automated recalibration of thermal models when anomalies or significant deviations are detected. Adjusts parameters to realign DT predictions with PT behavior."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element embedded in the styrofoam container controlled via Raspberry Pi using bang-bang strategy (binary on/off activation). No PWM modulation documented constrained by thermal capacity of heatbed."
]

instance IncubatorFanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Low-power fan ensuring uniform thermal distribution within the styrofoam box. Operation tied to heatbed activation with no independent control logic specified."
]

// C1: Physical Environment
instance IncubatorPhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical incubator system consisting of styrofoam-insulated container, heatbed, fan, and three temperature sensors. Operates in thermal regulation domain with primary objective of maintaining predefined internal temperature."
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, IncubatorFanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin instance integrating data, models, and algorithms for monitoring, simulation, and optimization of the incubator Physical Twin. Implements closed-loop architecture with self-adaptation capabilities."
    base:contains RaspberryPiController, RabbitMQMessageBroker, TwoParameterThermalModel, FourParameterThermalModel, RealTimeTemperatureData, CalibratedModelParameters
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the incubator DT is governed by a **bang-bang controller** deployed on the Raspberry Pi, which issues **binary control commands** to the PT’s actuators (heatbed and fan). The controller receives **temperature measurements** from the RabbitMQ server and executes a **state-based control logic** defined by four parameters: **LL (Lower Limit)**, **UL (Upper Limit)**, **H (Heating Duration)**, and **C (Waiting Duration)**. When the measured temperature falls below **LL**, the controller activates the **heatbed** for **H seconds**, then waits for **C seconds** to avoid overshoot. Conversely, if the temperature exceeds **UL**, the controller deactivates the heatbed. The **low-level driver** translates these commands into **actuator signals** (e.g., PWM for the heatbed) and enforces a **safe protocol** to prevent unsafe states (e.g., overheating). No explicit **command validation/authentication** (e.g., digital signatures, checksums) or **conflict resolution** mechanisms (e.g., priority arbitration) are documented. However, the **statechart-based controller** inherently ensures deterministic behavior, and the **RabbitMQ server** acts as a intermediary for command routing. The **feedback system** relies on the closed-loop nature of the controller, where sensor data continuously informs control decisions."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study operates with a temporal granularity defined by the control loop of the Raspberry Pi-based controller. The system periodically reads temperature data from the two internal sensors and one external sensor (Section 2.1), with the control loop parameters (e.g., heating duration and waiting duration) explicitly configured in Section 2.2.3. The calibration process for the thermal models (Model A with two parameters and Model B with four parameters) involves experimental data collection, but no specific synchronization frequency or latency requirements are documented. The only explicit temporal aspect is the periodic monitoring of temperature data by the low-level driver, which is relayed to the controller for real-time adjustments. No real-time visualization or Kalman filter-based anomaly detection is explicitly implemented or detailed in the provided documents, as these are referenced only as future goals in Section 5. The time scale for simulations (e.g., what-if scenarios) is not quantified in the provided text, but the system is designed to support co-simulations using historical or real-time data from the Physical Twin (PT)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator is structured around a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS) architecture**, integrating the following key components:
  1. **Physical Twin (PT)**: Comprising an insulated styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, and three temperature sensors (two internal, one external) (Section 2.1).
  2. **Cyber Twin (CT)**: Implemented on a Raspberry Pi, featuring:
     - A **low-level driver** for sensor data acquisition and actuator control (Section 2.2.2).
     - A **RabbitMQ communication server** for message relay between the driver and controller (Section 2.2.1).
     - A **temperature controller** (PID or similar) to regulate heating based on sensor feedback (Section 2.2.3).
  3. **Simulation Models**: Two calibrated thermal models (Model A and Model B) used for predictive analysis, with Model B preferred for its accuracy in thermal inertia modeling (Section 3).
  The system does not explicitly detail resource management or system-wide coordination mechanisms beyond the controller-driver-communication loop. The proposed self-adaptation loop (Section 5) is not implemented but would involve dynamic recalibration of Model B, Kalman filter updates, and controller reconfiguration—none of which are described in the provided documents. The constellation lacks explicit mention of orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or integration with external orchestration frameworks."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the incubator case study includes the following phases: **(1) Development Phase**: The DT is built by integrating **models** (e.g., thermal simulation models with 2 or 4 free parameters for temperature prediction) and **data** collected from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors (e.g., two internal temperature sensors, one external sensor). **(2) Calibration Phase**: Models are calibrated using **calibration algorithms** to update parameters (e.g., thermal inertia of the heatbed) based on real-time sensor data from the PT, ensuring alignment between the DT and PT. **(3) Operational Phase**: The DT employs **monitors** (e.g., safety and performance checks) to detect violations or suboptimal conditions, triggering **human or algorithmic interventions** (e.g., adjustments to the PT’s controller via Raspberry Pi). **(4) Evolution Phase**: The DT supports **reconfigurability** to adapt to changing environmental conditions (e.g., varying thermal loads) and **reduced maintenance** through predictive insights. **Lifecycle governance** is implied via version control (e.g., open-source repository updates) and iterative model refinement (e.g., transitioning from 2-parameter to 4-parameter models for improved accuracy)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is evaluated through **experimental validation**, where the **four-parameter model** outperformed the two-parameter model in predicting the **thermal inertia of the heatbed**, a critical factor for accurate temperature regulation. **Calibration** is performed using a **non-linear least squares solver** to minimize the discrepancy between simulated and measured temperatures, ensuring parameter estimates align with real-world behavior. Validation methods include:
  - **Comparative experiments**: Direct testing of both models against real-time sensor data to assess predictive accuracy.
  - **Parameter sensitivity analysis**: Implicit in the calibration process, though not explicitly detailed.
  Uncertainty sources are not quantified (e.g., no standard deviation or confidence intervals are provided), nor are specific **error detection/correction mechanisms** (e.g., isolation forests or machine learning classifiers) documented. The DT framework relies on **fault detectors** as a general capability (per Document 1), but no system-specific implementations (e.g., threshold-based alerts or adaptive recalibration) are described. Quality assurance is ensured through **iterative model refinement** and the use of **real-time sensor feedback**, though no formal verification protocols (e.g., model-in-the-loop testing) are mentioned."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for the incubator DT is centered around **RabbitMQ**, an open-source message broker implementing the **Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP)** for reliable communication. The system employs a **star topology** where the **Raspberry Pi** (hosting the controller, low-level driver, and RabbitMQ server) acts as the central node. Sensor data and control commands are transmitted via **RabbitMQ queues**, enabling **asynchronous, decoupled communication** between components. While the document does not specify **bandwidth/latency requirements**, the use of RabbitMQ implies **low-latency, high-throughput** capabilities suitable for real-time CPS applications. No explicit **redundancy mechanisms** (e.g., failover queues, replication) or **security protocols** (e.g., TLS/SSL encryption) are mentioned, though RabbitMQ supports these features by default. The **network architecture** is lightweight and **on-premise**, with no cloud integration described."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for the incubator DT is **edge-based**, with all critical components (controller, low-level driver, RabbitMQ server) running on a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B**. The system requires minimal **computational resources** (sufficient for real-time temperature control and message broker operations) and **storage** (limited to sensor logs and model parameters). No explicit **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., horizontal scaling, containerization) or **high-availability features** (e.g., clustering, failover) are documented. The deployment model is **monolithic**, with tightly coupled components, though RabbitMQ’s modular design allows for **future extensibility**. The **availability/reliability** is ensured by the **safe protocol** in the low-level driver and the deterministic nature of the bang-bang controller, but no formal **redundancy** (e.g., backup Pi instances) or **disaster recovery** procedures are described. RabbitMQ’s **on-premise deployment** is lightweight and **cloud-ready**, though the case study does not leverage cloud resources."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **safety-critical monitoring mechanisms** to ensure the physical twin operates within predefined bounds. Specifically, the system includes **monitors** that continuously evaluate whether the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment are performing **safely and optimally** (Document 1). Additionally, the low-level driver of the incubator system integrates a **safe protocol** to maintain the device in a **safe state** (implied by the context of safety-critical systems in Document 4). While explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication, or access control) are not detailed, the system prioritizes **fail-safe mechanisms** by leveraging calibration algorithms to update models dynamically, ensuring deviations from expected behavior trigger corrective actions (Document 1). The **Raspberry Pi-based controller** (Document 2) further enforces safety by interfacing with sensors (two internal temperature sensors and one external) to prevent overheating or unsafe thermal conditions, aligning with **safety-critical system considerations** for the incubator’s physical twin."
]
